notes: Canonical SKILL.md example. WPF/WinUI agents reach for `.Click +=` or a `.OnClick(...)` fluent chain on ButtonElement; Reactor exposes `onClick` as a factory parameter on Button(...) instead. Important: the agent's fallback to `.OnTapped(...)` is wrong — OnTapped is a gesture event with different input semantics. CS1061 on ButtonElement.OnClick. Not corpus-driven in this drop (cousin pattern is cluster C0102 / BorderElement, count=1); justified structurally via the WinUI 3 Button.Click event docs.

Fluent-method on Element<T>. WinUI 3 reaches for FrameworkElement.HorizontalAlignment property; Reactor uses .HAlign(HorizontalAlignment) shortcut. JaroWinkler distance ≈ 0.55, well below the Tier-2 0.70 floor — fuzzy match misses it. Corpus: cluster C0017 + others, ≈ 22 events combined. CS1061.
Sister to HAlign. Same fluent-shortcut shape; same Tier-2 fuzzy-match miss. CS1061.

Agent reaches for an English-plausible Theme token (AppBackground / DefaultBackground / WindowBackground / PageBackground). Reactor's canonical surface-background token is Theme.SolidBackground → SolidBackgroundFillColorBaseBrush. Top Phase-3 priority by corpus signal. Corpus: cluster C0019, freq 1.6%, 16 events. CS0117.
Same cluster (C0019) as AppBackground — same canonical Reactor target. CS0117.
Same cluster as AppBackground — desk-research extension; not yet in corpus but matches the WinUI mental model. CS0117.
Same cluster as AppBackground — desk-research extension. CS0117.
WinUI uses "LayerFillColorDefaultBrush" — Reactor exposes it as Theme.LayerFill (note: Fill, not Background). CS0117.
Common confusion — WinUI brushes end in `Brush`; Reactor tokens drop the suffix. CS0117.
Agent literally pastes the underlying resource key. Reactor abstracts the *FillColor*Default*Brush pattern away — use the short Theme.Accent token. CS0117.

WPF FrameworkElement.Margin takes a Thickness struct as a property. Reactor uses fluent .Margin(double uniform) / .Margin(h, v) / .Margin(l, t, r, b). CS0117 / CS1061 when agent writes `Margin = new Thickness(...)`.
Same pattern as Margin. CS0117 / CS1061.
WPF property assignment `FontWeight = FontWeights.Bold` becomes the fluent .Bold() on TextBlockElement / Heading / SubHeading. CS0117 (FontWeights doesn't ship) / CS1061.
Sister to Bold. .SemiBold() is TextBlockElement-only. CS0117 / CS1061.

WPF property assignment becomes a fluent method. Diag commonly comes through CS1061 on a *Element receiver.

notes: GridElement does not expose RowSpacing as a fluent method; corpus shows agents shifting to per-child .Margin(...) on Grid children. CS1061 — borderline structural rewrite (Class B documents the lookup; structural rewrites need a Class-A rule).
Sister to RowSpacing — same structural-rewrite pattern. CS1061.

On TextBlockElement specifically. Property in WPF/Silverlight; fluent method in Reactor. Real Reactor surface — TextBlockElement.TextAlignment(TextAlignment) — so the *suggestion* of TextAlignment is right, but only the fluent shape. CS1061.
